steps.title: Identify the subject, number, and required tense
steps.content: The subject is The word "robot" — singular. The sentence describes the word's first appearance in a 1920s play, a completed past event, so the verb must be singular and past tense.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice by rule
steps.content:
Rule: a verb must agree in number with its subject (subject-verb agreement), and tense must match the time frame of the action.
  • Choice A — 'were': plural past tense — violates subject-verb agreement with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice B — 'were being': plural past progressive — violates subject-verb agreement with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice C — 'was': singular simple past — agrees with the singular subject and correctly places the action in the completed past. ✓
  • Choice D — 'have been': plural present perfect — violates subject-verb agreement, and present perfect does not suit a specific completed historical event. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
